project.name: Archie
project.description: Static-publishable, multi-media exhibit annotation platform for authoring W3C WADM-compliant annotations in a browser Studio and publishing as a static IIIF site to GitHub Pages. Supports deep-zoom image annotation via OpenSeadragon and Annotorious, plus audio/video temporal annotation.

nodes.name: Annotation Authoring
nodes.summary: Core authoring domain for creating, editing, and deleting annotations (Notes) that target media objects, image regions, or time ranges. Each note is a W3C WADM-compliant AnnotationRecord appended to the version-DAG spine. Supports spatial selectors (rect/polygon) for images and temporal fragment selectors for audio/video.

businessRules: Every note has a unique LogicalId and RevId ULID
Notes are append-only, never deleted in place
Tombstone versions preserve citation integrity
Edits require a resolved single head (no concurrent conflict)
v1 shape vocabulary is rect + polygon only (FragmentSelector and SvgSelector)
Tags are bodies with purpose:tagging
crossDomainInteractions: Annotation Authoring feeds into Annotation Versioning (the log is the backbone)
Media Pipeline provides tile source resolution for spatial annotation targets
AV Transcript import creates supplementing notes from VTT/SRT input

nodes.name: Annotation Versioning and Merge
nodes.summary: Manages the append-only annotation log, version-DAG merge semantics, heads projection, and WADM serialization. Implements three-way merge without git via parent-DAG walking. The log is the authoritative source; heads and history pages are derived projections.

businessRules: Log is append-only and frozen after mutation
Version ordinal per logicalId is NOT unique under concurrency
RevId is collision-free and serves as the DAG node identifier
modifiedAt is an in-card tiebreaker ONLY, never auto-resolution
Plural heads are valid after unresolved concurrent merge
Tombstoned heads are excluded from projection
crossDomainInteractions: Annotation Versioning consumes the log from Annotation Authoring
Serialized WADM output feeds the Publishing domain
Merge supports async-zip collaboration workflow

nodes.name: Exhibit Management and IIIF Projection
nodes.summary: Defines the authoring domain model (Library, Exhibit, Object) and projects it into IIIF Presentation 3 formats. A Library contains self-contained Exhibits, each owning its Objects and their annotations. IIIF projections (Manifest, Collection, exhibits.json) enable interoperability with standard IIIF viewers.

businessRules: Objects are exhibit-nested, never shared across exhibits (ADR-0001)
Each Object projects to an IIIF Canvas with a painting AnnotationPage
Each Exhibit projects to an IIIF Manifest
Library projects to an IIIF Collection
Gallery source (exhibits.json) is separate from IIIF Collection
crossDomainInteractions: Exhibit model feeds the Publishing domain as the source data
IIIF projections contain references to annotation heads pages from Annotation Versioning
Gallery index is consumed by both Studio and Viewer

nodes.name: Media Pipeline
nodes.summary: Processes media sources for annotation: classifies image URLs into tile source descriptors (single JPEG vs IIIF image service), normalizes EXIF orientation for display, parses AV transcript files (VTT/SRT) into timestamped cue objects, and decodes image formats. All pure functions with no DOM or renderer dependencies.

businessRules: v1 tiling uses single responsive JPEG or external IIIF info.json
No wasm-vips tiling in v1 (ADR-0004)
EXIF orientation maps to flip/rotate transforms with dimension swapping
AV transcript import is import-only in v1, no client-side ASR
All 8 EXIF orientations are handled including transpose variants
crossDomainInteractions: Tile source resolution enables spatial annotation in Annotation Authoring
Transcript import feeds supplementing notes into the annotation log
EXIF normalization provides display-master dimensions for canvas sizing

nodes.name: Publishing
nodes.summary: Assembles the full published-site data tree through the Filesystem seam: IIIF Collection, Gallery source, per-Exhibit IIIF Manifest with annotations, and history sidecar. The zip is the architectural publish primitive. Supports round-trip via loadLibrary for reloading published sites from filesystem.

businessRules: Publish is an idempotent pure projection of Library + annotation logs
Zip is the universal publish primitive, per-host adapters are thin
Published tree includes collection.json, exhibits.json, manifest.json per exhibit
Per-canvas heads pages are written at paths the Manifest references
History sidecar enables reload and merge of published annotations
crossDomainInteractions: Publishing consumes Library model from Exhibit Management
Publishing serializes annotation logs from Annotation Versioning
Published output is consumed by the Viewer application
Zip export enables portable offline archives

nodes.name: Storage and Persistence
nodes.summary: Provides the Filesystem seam abstraction with three backends: MemoryFilesystem for tests and playground, OPFS adapter for sandboxed browser storage, and FSA adapter for Chromium folder access. Handles reading and writing annotation logs and IIIF projections through the seam.

businessRules: Filesystem seam is the source interface, backends are projections
OPFS is default for sandboxed storage
FSA folder opt-in enables git-native workflow
Memory backend serves tests and Playground
User-facing model is only Playground vs Project, never backend capability
crossDomainInteractions: Persistence underlies all domains as the storage layer
Publishing writes through the Filesystem seam
Persistence supports round-trip save/load of annotation logs

nodes.name: Linkability and Navigation
nodes.summary: Provides structured intra-Library link resolution and deep-link navigation contracts. Links are structured references (libraryId, exhibitSlug, noteLogicalId) resolved to published display URLs at publish time. Supports IIIF Content State encoding for cross-Library deep-linking.

businessRules: Intra-Library links use structured refs resolved at publish time
Cross-Library links reuse deep-link URL contract
Link index is built per-Exhibit for Viewer and Library-wide for Studio
Links are publish-time validated against the Library-wide index
Deep-link grammar is /{exhibitSlug}/#/a/{logicalId}
crossDomainInteractions: Link resolution happens at publish time in the Publishing domain
Deep links target annotation logicalIds from Annotation Versioning
Link index spans all exhibits in Exhibit Management

nodes.name: Schema Migration
nodes.summary: Provides a tldraw-pattern schema migration runner for versioned documents. Documents are stamped with a SCHEMA_VERSION at write time, and named migrations can be applied to bring old documents forward. Currently empty (v1 baseline) but the runner exists from day one.

businessRules: All persisted documents are stamped with schemaVersion
Migrations are ordered by target version and applied ascending
Migration runner is idempotent past current version
First real schema change just appends a migration entry
crossDomainInteractions: Migration is applied when writing any persisted document
Gallery source (exhibits.json) is stamped at publish time

nodes.name: Create Note
nodes.summary: Appends a brand-new annotation note to the log as version 1 (DAG root with null parent). Mints fresh ULID-based LogicalId and RevId, constructs the AnnotationRecord, and appends it to the immutable log.

nodes.name: Edit Note
nodes.summary: Appends a new version of an existing annotation note. Resolves the linear head of the note, carries forward unchanged fields, increments the version number, sets the parent pointer to the head's rev, and appends the new record.

nodes.name: Delete Note
nodes.summary: Appends a tombstone version of an annotation note. Validates the note exists and is not already tombstoned, then creates a deleted record with the previous target preserved for citation integrity.

nodes.name: Import AV Transcript
nodes.summary: Parses a WebVTT or SRT subtitle file into W3C annotation notes with supplementing motivation and FragmentSelector time-range targets. Each cue becomes a separate note appended to the log via appendNew.

nodes.name: Merge Annotation Logs
nodes.summary: Unions two annotation logs (local and incoming) by deduping on RevId, then per-logicalId classifies the relationship: identical (same rev), fast-forward (one is ancestor of other), or conflict (both advanced from a common ancestor). Provides the merge-base via parent-DAG walking.

nodes.name: Project Current State (Heads)
nodes.summary: Projects the append-only log to its currently-live head records. Collects all logical IDs, finds records that no other version references as parent, excludes tombstoned records, and returns them sorted deterministically.

nodes.name: Serialize to WADM
nodes.summary: Serializes the annotation log into W3C WADM-compliant AnnotationPages. Produces a heads page (current versions only, with archie:hasHistory and prov:wasRevisionOf link-outs) and a history sidecar (full version chain per logicalId with archie: DAG metadata for round-trip reconstruction).

nodes.name: Build Exhibit Model
nodes.summary: Defines the authoring domain model: a Library container with metadata, self-contained Exhibits with slugs and titles, and Objects with media sources, labels, types, and dimensions. Objects are nested within their owning Exhibit.

nodes.name: Project IIIF Manifest
nodes.summary: Projects an Exhibit into an IIIF Presentation 3 Manifest. Each Object becomes a Canvas with a painting AnnotationPage for the image/AV content and a reference to the Archie heads annotation page where viewer notes load.

nodes.name: Project IIIF Collection
nodes.summary: Projects a Library into an IIIF Presentation 3 Collection. Maps each Exhibit to a Manifest reference with language-mapped labels and optional cover thumbnails, preserving Library display order.

nodes.name: Build Gallery Index
nodes.summary: Generates the exhibits.json Gallery source from a Library. Projects each Exhibit into an ExhibitCard with slug, title, cover, description, and explicit display ordering. Computes whether to render the Gallery (collapses to single exhibit when only one). Stamps schema version for migratability.

nodes.name: Resolve Image Source
nodes.summary: Classifies an image source URL into a tile source descriptor. Recognizes IIIF info.json URLs, known image file extensions (JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, TIFF, SVG), or assumes a IIIF image service base URL and normalizes it.

nodes.name: Normalize EXIF Orientation
nodes.summary: Maps an EXIF Orientation tag (1-8) to the geometric transform (flipX, flipY, rotate) that produces an upright display-master image. Computes normalized display dimensions accounting for axis-swapping at 90/270 degree rotations.

nodes.name: Parse AV Transcript Cues
nodes.summary: Parses WebVTT or SRT subtitle/caption files into structured cue objects with start/end times in seconds and text content. Handles both VTT and SRT timestamp formats including comma and period decimal separators.

nodes.name: Publish Library to Filesystem
nodes.summary: Assembles the entire published-site data tree through the Filesystem seam. Writes collection.json, exhibits.json, per-Exhibit manifest.json, per-canvas heads annotation pages, and the history sidecar (index + per-logicalId history pages). Pure idempotent projection.

nodes.name: Export Library to Zip
nodes.summary: Packages the full published site into a .archie.zip byte array using the ZipFilesystem backend. This is the architectural publish primitive: everything runs through publishLibrary into a zip, and per-host adapters are thin wrappers.

nodes.name: Reload Published Site
nodes.summary: Inverse of publishLibrary: reconstructs the Library and per-exhibit annotation logs from a published site tree. Reads exhibits.json, per-Exhibit manifest.json, and the history sidecar. Recovers Object data from IIIF Manifest canvases.

nodes.name: Persist Annotations
nodes.summary: Writes the annotation log to a filesystem directory: the consumer heads page (heads.json), history sidecar (per-logicalId full version chains), and the history index mapping logicalIds to page URLs. Pure idempotent projection.

nodes.name: Load Annotations
nodes.summary: Reconstructs the full append-only annotation log from a filesystem directory. Reads the history index, loads each logicalId's history page, and deserializes the WADM annotations back into AnnotationRecords with full DAG metadata.

nodes.name: Select Storage Backend
nodes.summary: Instantiates the appropriate filesystem backend behind the Filesystem seam. MemoryFilesystem for tests and Playground (ephemeral), OPFS adapter for sandboxed browser storage, FSA adapter for Chromium folder access with git-native workflow.

nodes.name: Resolve Link and Deep Link
nodes.summary: Projects structured link targets to published display URLs. Builds note deep-link fragments (#/a/{logicalId}) and encodes IIIF Content State payloads for cross-Library deep-linking. Parses deep-link fragments on arrival to recover annotation targets.

nodes.name: Build Link Index
nodes.summary: Builds a Library-wide note index by projecting all exhibit annotation logs into a logicalId-to-exhibit mapping. Validates link targets against the index at publish time. Supports both per-Exhibit (Viewer read-time) and Library-wide (Studio author-time) indices.

nodes.name: Migrate Document Schema
nodes.summary: Applies ordered named migrations to bring a document forward from an older schema version. Reads the document's current schemaVersion, applies every migration whose target version exceeds it in ascending order, then stamps the current SCHEMA_VERSION. Idempotent.

nodes.name: Mint New IDs
nodes.summary: Generates a fresh ULID-based LogicalId (for stable logical identity) and RevId (for collision-free DAG node pointer). Injectable time and RNG for deterministic testing.

nodes.name: Construct AnnotationRecord
nodes.summary: Builds the AnnotationRecord struct with version=1, parent=null, target, optional body/motivation/layers, and the minted IDs.

nodes.name: Validate Input Fields
nodes.summary: Ensures required fields (target, lastEditor) are provided and optional fields are correctly typed before appending.

nodes.name: Append Record to Log
nodes.summary: Appends the new record to the frozen append-only log array, returning both the updated log and the created record.

nodes.name: Resolve Linear Head
nodes.summary: Finds the single head version of a logicalId by identifying records no other record references as parent. Throws on plural heads (unresolved concurrent merge) or absent note.

nodes.name: Validate Head Not Tombstoned
nodes.summary: Checks that the existing note is not deleted. Throws an error if attempting to edit a tombstoned note.

nodes.name: Carry Forward Unchanged Fields
nodes.summary: Copies unchanged fields (body, motivation, target, layers) from the head record when the edit input does not provide replacements.

nodes.name: Increment Version with Parent Pointer
nodes.summary: Sets version = head.version + 1 and parent = head.rev, forming a linear version chain in the DAG. Mints a new RevId.

nodes.name: Append New Version Record
nodes.summary: Appends the new versioned record to the frozen log, completing the edit operation.

nodes.name: Resolve Linear Head
nodes.summary: Finds the single head version of the logicalId to determine the current state before deletion.

nodes.name: Validate Not Already Deleted
nodes.summary: Checks the head record's deleted flag. Throws if the note is already tombstoned.

nodes.name: Create Tombstone Record
nodes.summary: Builds a new record with deleted=true, preserving the target for citation/dereference but carrying no body.

nodes.name: Append Tombstone to Log
nodes.summary: Appends the tombstone record to the frozen log. The deletion is recorded as a new version, never erasing history.

nodes.name: Parse Transcript Format
nodes.summary: Normalizes line endings (CRLF, CR to LF) and detects VTT or SRT cue blocks separated by blank lines.

nodes.name: Extract Timestamp Cues
nodes.summary: Finds arrow-timestamp lines (-->), parses start/end times in HH:MM:SS.mmm format to seconds, and extracts the associated text.

nodes.name: Convert Cues to W3C Notes
nodes.summary: Maps each cue to a W3C supplementing annotation with TextualBody and FragmentSelector time-range target (t=start,end).

nodes.name: Append Notes to Log
nodes.summary: Sequentially appends each cue-derived supplementing note to the annotation log via appendNew, with distinct time-ordered IDs.

nodes.name: Union Two Logs With Dedup
nodes.summary: Combines two annotation logs into one by iterating both, tracking seen RevIds, and only appending unseen records.

nodes.name: Classify Heads Per LogicalId
nodes.summary: Determines the merge relationship for each logicalId: present only in local, only in incoming, or in both. For both, classifies as identical, fast-forward, or conflict.

nodes.name: Compute Merge Base via DAG Walk
nodes.summary: Walks the parent-DAG (via RevIds, the collision-free node IDs) to find the nearest common ancestor of two competing heads.

nodes.name: Classify Merge (identical/fast-forward/conflict)
nodes.summary: Determines if two heads are identical (same rev), fast-forward (one is ancestor of other), or conflict (both diverged from common ancestor).

nodes.name: Apply In-Card Tiebreaker
nodes.summary: Uses modifiedAt as an in-card tiebreaker ONLY (never auto-resolution). Prefers the later timestamp as UI suggestion inside a conflict card.

nodes.name: Collect All Logical IDs
nodes.summary: Gathers all unique logicalIds from the annotation log into a set to iterate over for head computation.

nodes.name: Find Head Records
nodes.summary: For each logicalId, finds records that no other version references as parent. Returns plural heads after unresolved concurrent merge.

nodes.name: Exclude Tombstones and Sort
nodes.summary: Filters out tombstoned (deleted) head records from the projection, then sorts remaining heads deterministically by logicalId then rev.

nodes.name: Build Citation ID Map
nodes.summary: Maps every record's RevId to its citation id (logicalId/version). Disambiguates concurrent version collisions by appending ~{rev} suffix to the lexicographically later RevId.

nodes.name: Construct Heads Page WADM Annotations
nodes.summary: Converts head records to W3C Annotation objects with archie:hasHistory (link to history page) and prov:wasRevisionOf (link to prior version).

nodes.name: Build History Sidecar Pages
nodes.summary: Produces per-logicalId AnnotationPages with the full version chain, each annotated with archie: DAG metadata (logicalId, rev, version, parent, lastEditor, deleted) for round-trip reconstruction.

nodes.name: Embed Archie DAG Metadata
nodes.summary: Annotates WADM annotations with archie: extension fields (logicalId, rev, version, parent, lastEditor, deleted, layers) for pure-WADM consumers to ignore and Archie readers to reconstruct.

nodes.name: Define Library Container
nodes.summary: Creates the top-level Library with id, optional title and summary. Serves as the unit of publication, projecting to an IIIF Collection.

nodes.name: Configure Exhibit with Metadata
nodes.summary: Defines an Exhibit with id, slug, title, optional summary and cover. Self-contained unit that owns its Objects and projects to an IIIF Manifest.

nodes.name: Attach Objects with Media Types
nodes.summary: Defines media Objects with source URL, label, mediaType (image/sound/video), dimensions, duration, and format. Each projects to an IIIF Canvas.

nodes.name: Create Manifest with IIIF Context
nodes.summary: Builds the IIIF Presentation 3 Manifest container with @context, id, type=Manifest, label, and optional summary from the Exhibit data.

nodes.name: Map Objects to Canvases
nodes.summary: Converts each AObject into an IIIF Canvas with calculated id, label, dimensions, and duration based on media type (image/sound/video).

nodes.name: Add Painting Annotation Page
nodes.summary: Creates the painting AnnotationPage for each Canvas, referencing the image/AV source as the body with type-specific properties (format, dimensions, duration).

nodes.name: Link Heads Annotation Page Reference
nodes.summary: Adds an annotations reference on each Canvas pointing to the Archie heads annotation page (canvas/{objId}/annotations.json) where viewer notes load.

nodes.name: Create IIIF Collection
nodes.summary: Builds the IIIF Presentation 3 Collection container with @context, id, type=Collection, and label from the Library metadata.

nodes.name: Map Exhibits to Manifest References
nodes.summary: Converts each Exhibit into a IIIF Manifest reference item with id pointing to the exhibit's manifest.json and language-mapped label.

nodes.name: Include Cover Thumbnails
nodes.summary: Attaches optional thumbnail references to Manifest items when the Exhibit has a cover image URL.

nodes.name: Project Library into Exhibit Cards
nodes.summary: Converts each Library Exhibit into an ExhibitCard with slug, title, optional cover/description, and explicit ordering index.

nodes.name: Compute Gallery Render Threshold
nodes.summary: Determines whether the Gallery should render: skipped only when there is exactly one Exhibit and no Library framing (title/summary).

nodes.name: Stamp Schema Version
nodes.summary: Stamps the exhibits.json document with the current SCHEMA_VERSION to ensure future migratability.

nodes.name: Classify Source URL
nodes.summary: Tests the source URL against known patterns: IIIF info.json, image file extensions, or IIIF image service base URL.

nodes.name: Check Known Image Extensions
nodes.summary: Matches source URL against known image extensions (jpeg, png, webp, avif, gif, tiff, svg) to classify as a single-image source.

nodes.name: Fall Back to IIIF Service
nodes.summary: Treats unrecognized URLs as IIIF image service base URLs and normalizes by appending /info.json.

nodes.name: Look Up EXIF Orientation Tag
nodes.summary: Maps EXIF orientation value (1-8) to its base transform from a lookup table. Unknown values default to identity (orientation 1).

nodes.name: Compute Flip/Rotation Transform
nodes.summary: Determines the complete geometric transform (flipX, flipY, rotate, swapsAxes) that produces an upright display-master from the orientation data.

nodes.name: Calculate Normalized Dimensions
nodes.summary: Swaps width and height when the transform involves a 90 or 270 degree rotation (axes swap). Returns the display-master dimensions.

nodes.name: Normalize Line Endings
nodes.summary: Converts CRLF and CR line endings to LF for consistent parsing. Handles both VTT and SRT interchange formats.

nodes.name: Split into Cue Blocks
nodes.summary: Divides the normalized transcript into blocks separated by blank lines. Each block potentially contains a timestamp line and text.

nodes.name: Parse Timestamps and Extract Text
nodes.summary: Finds arrow-timestamp lines (start --> end), parses HH:MM:SS.mmm to seconds, and extracts the associated cue text. Handles both period and comma decimal separators.

nodes.name: Write Collection JSON
nodes.summary: Writes the IIIF Collection (collection.json) to the filesystem root. Projects Library to Collection with Manifest references.

nodes.name: Write Exhibits Gallery Source
nodes.summary: Writes the Gallery source (exhibits.json) to the filesystem root with schema version stamping for migratability.

nodes.name: Write Per-Exhibit Manifests
nodes.summary: For each Exhibit, creates a slug-named directory and writes manifest.json (IIIF Manifest with all Object Canvases).

nodes.name: Write Per-Canvas Heads Pages
nodes.summary: Projects the annotation log to heads, groups by canvas target, and writes per-object annotation pages at paths the Manifest references.

nodes.name: Write History Sidecar Index
nodes.summary: Writes the history index.json mapping each logicalId to its history page URL. Serves as the merge/reload load target.

nodes.name: Write Per-LogicalId History Pages
nodes.summary: Writes each logicalId's full version chain as a separate JSON AnnotationPage, enabling citation dereference and log reconstruction.

nodes.name: Create ZipFilesystem
nodes.summary: Instantiates a ZipFilesystem backend implementing the Filesystem seam, which accumulates writes into an in-memory zip archive.

nodes.name: Run Publish Through Zip Backend
nodes.summary: Executes the full publishLibrary pipeline with the ZipFilesystem, writing the entire site tree into the in-memory zip.

nodes.name: Return Zip Byte Array
nodes.summary: Finalizes the zip archive and returns the complete .archie.zip as a Uint8Array for download or transfer.

nodes.name: Read Exhibits Gallery Source
nodes.summary: Reads exhibits.json from the published filesystem tree to recover the Library-level exhibit ordering and metadata.

nodes.name: Read Per-Exhibit Manifest
nodes.summary: For each exhibit, reads its manifest.json to recover Canvas structure and object metadata.

nodes.name: Reconstruct Objects from Manifest
nodes.summary: Runs objectsFromManifest to reverse-map IIIF Canvases back into AObject types with source, label, media type, and dimensions.

nodes.name: Read Annotations from History
nodes.summary: Reads the history sidecar directory, loads the index, and reads each logicalId's full version chain from its history page.

nodes.name: Reconstruct Full Annotation Log
nodes.summary: Deserializes all history page WADM annotations back into AnnotationRecords by extracting archie: DAG metadata, rebuilding the full version-DAG log.

nodes.name: Serialize Heads Page
nodes.summary: Converts the annotation log to a heads page W3C AnnotationPage with archie:hasHistory and prov:wasRevisionOf link-outs.

nodes.name: Serialize History Sidecar
nodes.summary: Generates the history sidecar with per-logicalId AnnotationPages containing the full version chain and DAG metadata.

nodes.name: Write Index JSON
nodes.summary: Writes the history index.json file mapping each logicalId to its history page URL.

nodes.name: Write Per-LogicalId History Files
nodes.summary: Writes each logicalId's full version chain AnnotationPage as a separate JSON file named {logicalId}.json.

nodes.name: Read History Index from Filesystem
nodes.summary: Reads the history/index.json file to discover all logicalIds and their history page URLs.

nodes.name: Read Each LogicalId History Page
nodes.summary: Iterates the index and loads each logicalId's history AnnotationPage JSON file.

nodes.name: Deserialize WADM to Records
nodes.summary: Parses each history page's WADM annotations back into AnnotationRecords by extracting archie: DAG metadata fields.

nodes.name: Reconstruct Full DAG
nodes.summary: Aggregates all records from all history pages into a single AnnotationLog, rebuilding the complete version-DAG.

nodes.name: Instantiate MemoryFilesystem
nodes.summary: Creates an in-memory filesystem backend for unit tests and the Playground ephemeral working store.

nodes.name: Use OPFS Adapter
nodes.summary: Instantiates the Origin Private File System adapter for sandboxed browser storage. Default for all browsers for working data.

nodes.name: Use FSA Adapter
nodes.summary: Instantiates the File System Access API adapter for Chromium folder access. Enables git-native workflow with visible on-disk files.

nodes.name: Build Structured Link Target
nodes.summary: Constructs a LinkTarget with exhibitSlug, optional noteLogicalId, rangeId, or xywh region. Supports both intra-Library and cross-Library references.

nodes.name: Construct Note Deep Link Fragment
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical note deep-link fragment (#/a/{logicalId}) with optional xywh parameter for region targeting.

nodes.name: Encode IIIF Content State
nodes.summary: Encodes an annotation reference into a IIIF Content State (base64url JSON-LD) for standardized cross-Library deep-linking.

nodes.name: Parse Deep Link on Arrival
nodes.summary: Parses a note deep-link fragment back into its logicalId and optional xywh parameters for viewport navigation.

nodes.name: Index All LogicalIds Across Exhibits
nodes.summary: Scans all exhibit annotation logs and builds a logicalId-to-exhibitSlug mapping for the Library-wide note index.

nodes.name: Validate Link Targets
nodes.summary: Checks that a LinkTarget's noteLogicalId or exhibitSlug exists in the Library-wide index, ensuring publish-time link integrity.

nodes.name: Resolve to Published Display URL
nodes.summary: Projects a structured LinkTarget to its published display URL using the pinned base URL and exhibit slug grammar.

nodes.name: Read Document Version
nodes.summary: Extracts the schemaVersion field from the document. Returns 0 for unversioned documents.

nodes.name: Apply Ordered Migrations
nodes.summary: Iterates migrations in ascending order and applies each whose target version exceeds the document's current version.

nodes.name: Stamp Updated Schema Version
nodes.summary: Ensures the document's schemaVersion is at least SCHEMA_VERSION after all migrations are applied.

edges.description: Create/edit/delete operations append AnnotationRecords to the version-DAG log managed by Annotation Versioning
Heads projection and WADM serialization produce the annotation pages published by the Publishing domain
Library/Exhibit model is the source data for the publish pipeline's site tree assembly
Tile source resolution enables spatial annotation targets; transcript import feeds supplementing notes into the annotation log
Persistence reads/writes annotation logs through the Filesystem seam
Publish pipeline writes through the Filesystem seam; reload reads from it
Link targets are resolved to published display URLs at publish time
Gallery source (exhibits.json) is stamped with schema version at publish time
Schema version stamping and migration apply to all persisted documents
